title: Recast the target as a discrete-time hazard specialist
summary: Pit-next-lap is a one-step event hazard.
rationale: Pit-next-lap is a one-step event hazard. Discrete-time survival models are designed for per-interval event probabilities and often outperform plain binary framing when risk rises sharply near event time.
implementation_hint: Train a specialist on stacked horizon labels such as pit in 1, 2, or 3 laps, or a discrete-time hazard model on current row with causal features only. Blend its next-lap hazard with the main classifier rather than replacing the classifier outright.
expected_effect: Better monotone event-timing behavior near stint end; may improve ranking around borderline rows where binary trees flatten probabilities.
risk: If horizons are generated carelessly, label overlap can make CV optimistic; all horizon-derived labels and folds must respect race-driver chronology.
